findings.title: Skill body word count outside target range (target: 1,500-2,000)
findings.snippet:
# Refactor Plan — Generate Refactoring Plans from Analysis

Generate refactoring plans from existing analysis findings without re-scanning the codebase.
findings.description: The refactor-plan SKILL.md body contains approximately 1,205 words, below the 1,500-word target minimum. The skill delegates most work to the refactoring-planner agent, but the body's flag documentation, edge cases, and error handling sections are thin. The flag-behavior.md reference file exists but the body provides insufficient inline summary of the Phase 1–3 execution flow.
findings.recommendation: Expand the critic feedback loop documentation and inline a condensed summary of how each flag affects agent behavior. The references/flag-behavior.md can retain full detail. Target adding approximately 300 words to the Phase 2 and edge-case sections.

findings.title: Skill body word count outside target range (target: 1,500-2,000)
findings.snippet:
# Scan Security

## Purpose

Analyze the codebase for security vulnerabilities aligned with the OWASP Top 10...
findings.description: The scan-security SKILL.md body contains approximately 1,415 words, below the 1,500-word target minimum. The skill covers 11 steps but Steps 6 (path traversal), 7 (SSRF), and 8 (sensitive logging) are compressed to 3–4 lines each. The plugin security steps (P1–P4) are also terse and lack example grep command patterns.
findings.recommendation: Expand Steps 6–8 with additional per-language grep pattern examples (Python, TypeScript, C#, Go). Add one concrete example grep command per plugin security step (P1–P4). Target adding approximately 90 words to reach the 1,500-word floor.

findings.title: Skill body word count outside target range (target: 1,500-2,000)
findings.snippet:
# Scan Quality

## Purpose

Evaluate code quality across three sub-areas: **Code Health**, **Tech Debt**, and **Performance**.
findings.description: The scan-quality SKILL.md body contains approximately 2,333 words, above the 2,000-word target maximum. The skill covers 16 steps spanning code health, tech debt, and performance analysis. Performance-specific grep patterns in Steps 11–15 are detailed reference material that Claude does not need on every activation.
findings.recommendation: Create a references/ directory and extract the performance grep patterns (Steps 11–15) to references/performance-patterns.md. Update the body: 'Read references/performance-patterns.md when analyzing N+1 queries, pagination, memory patterns, frontend rendering, or caching.' This brings the body into the 1,700–1,900 word range.

findings.title: Skill body exceeds 2,000 words with no references/ directory
findings.snippet:
# Scan Quality

## Purpose

Evaluate code quality across three sub-areas...
findings.description: The scan-quality skill has 2,333 body words but no references/ directory. The 16-step workflow loads entirely into context on every invocation. Performance analysis patterns (Steps 11–15) and deprecated API lists (Step 8) are reference material — they serve as look-up data rather than procedural instructions — and belong at Level 3 in the progressive disclosure hierarchy.
findings.recommendation: Create skills/scan-quality/references/ with at least: performance-patterns.md (Steps 11–15 grep patterns by language) and deprecated-apis.md (Step 8 deprecated pattern lists). In the SKILL.md body, add conditional references: 'Read references/performance-patterns.md when the scan reaches data access, rendering, or caching analysis' and 'Read references/deprecated-apis.md when scanning for deprecated API usage in Step 8.'

findings.title: Skill body word count outside target range (target: 1,500-2,000)
findings.snippet:
# Analyze Codebase — Orchestrator

Comprehensive codebase analysis across 4 standard dimensions (structure, quality, security, testing)...
findings.description: The analyze-codebase SKILL.md body contains approximately 2,070 words, slightly above the 2,000-word target ceiling. The skill correctly uses progressive disclosure via references/model-resolution.md and references/plugin-mode.md. The overage comes from the detailed flag alias table, per-stage model override syntax, and repeated model-passing instructions across Stages 3–8.
findings.recommendation: Extract the --dimensions alias mapping table and the --model per-stage syntax examples into a references/ file (e.g., references/flag-reference.md), or compress the model-passing instructions in Stages 3–8 into a single note: 'Pass model: MODEL_MAP.{stage} to each dispatched agent.' Target reducing by 70–100 words.

findings.title: Large inline code block should be extracted to a supporting file
findings.snippet:
```text
# Standard plugin with skills and agents
my-plugin/
├── .claude-plugin/
│   └── plugin.json
├── skills/
│   └── my-skill/
│       └── SKILL.md
...
```
findings.description: The scan-manifest-structure SKILL.md contains a fenced text block spanning approximately 31 lines (lines 69–121), showing acceptable and unacceptable directory structure examples. This exceeds the 30-line threshold for large inline content blocks. The examples are reference material (comparison diagrams) rather than procedural instructions, making them a candidate for Level 3 progressive disclosure.
findings.recommendation: Extract the directory structure examples (both acceptable and unacceptable patterns) into references/structure-examples.md. Instruct the skill to Read it conditionally in Step 3: 'Read references/structure-examples.md for acceptable and unacceptable plugin directory structure diagrams.' This reduces the body's prose-to-example ratio and enables selective loading.
